summary:
Fixed TS DTO drift from Rust domain types. The 3 core entity types (Invoice, InvoiceLineItem, Vendor) in api.ts were already aligned with Rust. Made `created_by` and `supporting_documents` required (matching Rust's non-Option types). OrganizationBranding in api.ts correctly uses camelCase (matching Rust's `#[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")]`).

The critical fix was `stores/auth.ts` OrganizationTheme type - was using camelCase (`presetId`, `customColors`, `gradientConfig`, `enabledForAllUsers`, `allowUserOverride`) but Rust OrganizationTheme has no `rename_all` attribute, so it serializes as snake_case. Fixed all field names to snake_case. Also fixed `stores/theme.ts` OrganizationThemeConfig similarly.

Updated all consumers: organization-theme-provider.tsx, organization-theme-settings.tsx, theme-settings.tsx, settings/page.tsx, settings/theme/page.tsx. The importThemeConfig function now handles both old camelCase and new snake_case keys for backward compatibility with persisted localStorage data.

Updated dto-alignment.test.ts with correct OrganizationBranding camelCase test and Invoice samples including required `created_by`/`supporting_documents`.

Stashed unrelated changes to home/page.tsx before committing.
decisions_made: OrganizationBranding in api.ts stays camelCase because Rust uses rename_all = camelCase
auth.ts OrganizationTheme fixed to snake_case matching Rust serialization
theme.ts OrganizationThemeConfig fixed to snake_case matching Rust serialization
Branding sub-object in auth.ts stays camelCase (wraps OrganizationBranding which uses camelCase)
importThemeConfig accepts both old camelCase and new snake_case for backward compatibility
ThemeState internal fields (presetId, customColors) left as camelCase - store-internal, not DTOs
open_questions: Need build verification: `cd apps/web && npx next build` should pass
DTO alignment test needs to be run
next_steps: Run `cd apps/web && npx next build` to verify no TS type errors
